release.title: R5: Add operator-facing status and doctor tooling for loop recovery
release.summary: Ship the single high-priority goal `Add operator-facing status and doctor tooling for loop recovery` as a standalone bundled release.

brief.objective: Give operators a first-class way to inspect loop state and recover from blockers.
brief.target_user_value: Operators can answer where the loop is stuck and what to run next without reading raw state files.
brief.why_now: After telemetry, state consistency, report trust, and PM release planning quality, the main UX gap is day-to-day recovery and inspection.
brief.packaging_rationale: Status and doctor belong together because one explains current loop state while the other explains how to recover.
scope_in: Document status and doctor in README, protocol, and installer guidance
scope_out: No new validation or committee gates
release_acceptance: loop-status.py prints session, release, goal, and gate status
brief.launch_story: This release gives operators a practical cockpit for the loop: one command to inspect state, one command to recover.

goal_titles: Add operator-facing status and doctor tooling for loop recovery

task_iterations.goal: Add operator-facing status and doctor tooling for loop recovery

research_findings: Usage-log analysis shows operators need a faster way to inspect live loop state without reading raw JSON.
committee_feedback: Product, architecture, and operator councils aligned on shipping lightweight read-only operator recovery tooling.
committee_decision: Approve a single bundled release for loop-status, loop-doctor, and operator-facing guidance.
reflection_notes: Operator recovery should stay read-only in the first release to preserve trust and avoid hidden automation.

research_gate.summary: Usage-log analysis shows operators need a clearer way to inspect live loop state and recover from blockers without reading raw JSON state files.
open_gaps: Status and doctor output should stay concise enough for day-to-day use.

product_council.summary: This release closes the highest remaining UX gap after telemetry and report trust by giving operators an explicit inspection and recovery surface.

architecture_council.summary: Standalone status and doctor scripts fit the existing repo-local script boundary and avoid coupling operator recovery UX into publishing or validation flows.

operator_council.summary: Operators need a simple answer to where the loop is blocked and what command to run next; status and doctor provide that without forcing raw state inspection.

delivery_secretary.summary: Ship a bundled workflow release that adds inspection and recovery commands plus documentation and installer guidance.
delivery_secretary.next_action: Run full validation, then publish the task and bundled release.

audit_secretary.summary: Decision is backed by usage-log findings and durable state coverage; no escalation is required if operator tools stay read-only.
audit_secretary.decision_record: Approve the operator recovery release with scope limited to loop-status, loop-doctor, validation coverage, and operator-facing docs.

scope_decision.why_selected: Operator recovery is the remaining UX gap after telemetry, state consistency, report trust, and PM release planning quality were delivered.
scope_in: Add loop-status.py for structured loop inspection.
Add loop-doctor.py for actionable recovery diagnostics.
Document operator recovery commands in README, protocol, and installer guidance.
scope_out: Do not add new validation gates or committee roles.
assumptions: Existing durable state is sufficient to explain operator-facing loop health.
risks: Operator output could become noisy if status and doctor expose too much raw state.
required_validation: validate-kit.py must cover loop-status.py and loop-doctor.py.
run-full-validation.py must remain green after adding operator tooling.
stop_conditions: Stop if status or doctor require mutating state to be useful.
dissent: Keep the first release of operator tooling read-only and avoid auto-repair behavior.
scope_decision.next_action: Validate the new operator tooling and publish the bundled release if all gates pass.

critique: The release is tightly scoped, durable, and easy to validate.
minimum_fixes_required: Keep operator tooling read-only and block publish if validation regresses.

escalation.recommended_action: Proceed to validation and publish if the new tools remain green.
